signals.title: USD/JPY — Carry + Intervention Risk + Retail Divergence
signals.text: USD/JPY at 161.55, holding above the 160.00 intervention watch level. The 264bp Fed-BoJ OIS carry premium (SOFR 3.62% vs TONA 0.98%) continues to. higher, despite JPY being 'knocked down twice' per recent news. COT specs are NET SHORT -112,092 (long%=29.7%), indicating a crowded carry trade. Retail sentiment is extremely short at 86% (Myfxbook), creating a strong institutional vs. retail divergence. Intervention risk remains elevated on any sustained push above 160.00, especially with the crowded spec short. Watch for BoJ verbal warnings or direct action if 162.00 is tested.

signals.title: NZD/USD — G10 Laggard + Price Action + Volatility
signals.text: NZD/USD at 0.5709, trading to new lows for the year, confirming its status as the weakest G10 currency this week (NZD 1W avg: -0.612% vs G10 peers). The pair faces a significant carry disadvantage with a 150bp Fed-RBNZ spread favoring USD (SOFR 3.62% vs OCR-overnight 2.25%). Options market shows elevated hedging demand with VRP at +2.3% (IV 7.1% vs HV30 4.8%). COT specs are NET SHORT -22,454 (long%=18.4%), an extreme short position. Retail sentiment is extremely long at 94% (Myfxbook), setting up a potential capitulation if price extends lower. The RBNZ's next meeting on 8 Jul is a key catalyst for any shift in bias.

signals.title: EUR/USD — ECB Stance + USD Strength + Options Skew
signals.text: EUR/USD at 1.1432, falling as the USD gains on 'Iran optimism' and ECB's Lagarde tempers hawkish expectations. The 144bp OIS carry spread (SOFR 3.62% vs ESTR 2.18%) structurally favors USD. COT specs are NET SHORT -40,372 (long%=39.1%), indicating a bearish bias. The options market shows a mild put skew (EURUSD 25d RR: -0.61), suggesting demand for downside protection despite neutral speculative positioning. Fed's Goolsbee's hawkish comments reinforce USD strength, limiting EUR/USD upside. Watch for the next ECB meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 23 Jul for any shift in forward guidance.

signals.title: AUD/USD — Business Activity + Negative CESI + Volatility
signals.text: AUD/USD at 0.6985, under pressure following news of Australian business activity steadying but new orders falling. The AUD CESI is at -8 (moderate negative bias), indicating data consistently disappointing expectations. Despite a 73bp OIS carry premium favoring AUD (AONIA 4.35% vs SOFR 3.62%), the pair is struggling. Options market shows an elevated vol premium with VRP at +6.0% (IV 13.8% vs HV30 7.8%) and a moderate put skew (AUDUSD 25d RR: -0.90), signaling strong demand for downside protection. COT specs are NET LONG +38,548 (long%=70.2%), suggesting a crowded long position vulnerable to further negative data. The next RBA meeting (mkt: 100% hold priced) on 11 Aug is the next major event.
